name: The parable of the sower
notes: The parable of the sower with its explanation, given in all three synoptics. The shared core runs closely parallel: the same setting by the sea (Luke's is briefer), the four soils, the call to hear, the purpose-of-parables saying drawn from Isaiah, and the fourfold interpretation matched part-for-part. Each account keeps its own vantage: Matthew alone quotes Esaias at length and adds the beatitude on seeing eyes; Mark stresses 'know ye not this parable' and the lusts of other things; Luke compresses the soils yet uniquely interprets the good ground as 'an honest and good heart' that keeps the word and brings forth fruit 'with patience,' and frames falling away as a 'time of temptation.' Only beats with genuinely shared wording or content are linked; each Evangelist's distinctive emphases read in place and are left as a matter for further study.
